G.I. Joe: Retaliation (2013)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $122,523,060Details
International Box Office $253,217,645
Worldwide Box Office $375,740,705
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $17,099,822
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $20,209,295
Total $37,309,117
Further financial details...

Synopsis

In this sequel, the G.I. JOE team is not only fighting their mortal enemy COBRA; they are forced to contend with threats from within the government that jeopardize their very existence.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$40,501,814 (33.1% of total gross)
Legs:3.03 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:32.6% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$140,000,000 (worldwide box office is 2.7 times production budget)
Theater counts:3,719 opening theaters/3,734 max. theaters, 5.7 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $180,243,025

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: March 27th, 2013 (Wide) by Paramount Pictures
March 27th, 2013 (IMAX) by Paramount Pictures
International Releases: March 27th, 2013 (Wide) (United Kingdom)
March 27th, 2013 (Wide) (Thailand)
March 27th, 2013 (Wide) (Taiwan)
March 27th, 2013 (Wide) (Sweden)
March 27th, 2013 (Wide) (Spain)
Video Release: July 30th, 2013 by Paramount Home Video
MPA Rating: PG-13 for intense sequences of combat violence and martial arts action throughout, and for brief sensuality and language.
(Rating bulletin 2262, 3/6/2013)
Running Time: 110 minutes
Franchise: G.I. Joe
Film format: 3-D, 3-D - Post-production Conversion, IMAX: DMR
Filming location (country): United States
Plot point: Framed, Government Corruption, Stolen Identity, Terrorism
Release detail: Delayed Release
Subgenre: Action Adventure
Source:Based on Toy
Genre:Action
Production Method:Animation/Live Action
Creative Type:Contemporary Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: Paramount Pictures, Metro-Goldwyn-Mayer Pictures, Skydance Productions, Hasbro Studios, di Bonaventura Pictures
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English
